title: ⛳ Parameter Golf Live AI Commentary ⛳ + Analysis / Ideas | every 10 minutes
key_insights: The n-gram eval cache (BackoffNgramMixer) is the dominant technique, providing up to ~0.97 BPB improvement over neural-only models. The latest Frozen N-gram Oracle + learned expert gate approach reached 0.1663 BPB pending. Key blockers identified include seq curriculum breaking SWA and EMA causing a 0.14 BPB quantization gap on SWA-stacks.

title: Invalid submissions due to information leakage during TTT
key_insights: Many submissions implemented TTT incorrectly by adapting on all eval tokens before scoring, which is equivalent to training on the eval set and constitutes information leakage. Legal TTT must score each token t before adapting on tokens ≤t. At least 9 PRs were flagged as potentially invalid, and this issue triggered the March 24 enforcement sweep that closed 15+ submissions.

title: Unofficial Leaderboard
key_insights: Early unofficial leaderboard snapshot documenting key winning techniques with quantified BPB impacts: sliding window eval (-0.034), MLP 3x (-0.019), 10-layer mixed precision (-0.01), long-context training (-0.01), fp16 tied embedding (-0.007), Muon tuning (-0.005), LoRA TTT (-0.004). Val-only training (memorization) achieves 1.0149 BPB but is flagged separately.

title: [Tips] for newbies like myself
key_insights: Practical infrastructure guide for RunPod: torch.compile takes 3-4 minutes but doesn't count against the 600s training budget, network volumes must match datacenter, and Blackwell GPUs (RTX Pro 6000) may have torch.compile issues. Pre-tokenized SP-4096 and SP-8192 data is publicly available on HuggingFace, saving significant setup time.

title: flash_attn_interface (FA3) missing from runpod/parameter-golf:latest image
key_insights: The official RunPod template is missing flash_attn_interface (FA3) and flash_attn (FA2), causing top submissions that use FA3 without fallbacks to crash on the evaluation environment. A try/except fallback to PyTorch SDPA is recommended as a workaround, though it is ~10% slower than FA3 on H100.

title: Live Leaderboard for Parameter Golf
key_insights: A community-built live leaderboard at parameter-golf.github.io auto-updates every 30 minutes, showing rankings, scores, and linked PRs. Val-only submissions are excluded by default, and filtering for closed PRs was requested to reduce noise.

title: Proposal: share weights by default
key_insights: Proposal to publish trained model weights (e.g., on HuggingFace) to avoid redundant retraining, enable reproducibility verification, and support downstream research like interpretability and distillation. Maintainer expressed interest; files are small enough to host on GitHub directly.

title: [QUESTION] Can we use zstandard instead of zlib?
key_insights: Confirmed by organizer (@0hq) that zstandard (zstd) compression is allowed as a replacement for zlib in artifact compression, as all imports and methods are permitted provided compute, time, and eval limits are respected.

title: Title: Record submission: FarnsworthEngine v1 — val_bpb=1.1303 (mean 1.1313, 3 seeds)
key_insights: Record submission request for PR #254 achieving mean 1.1313 BPB across 3 seeds using TTT + 11-layer Int6 MLP3x + SmearGate + BigramHash + OrthoInit + SWA + FA3 + sliding window stride=64. However, this submission was later flagged in issue #402 as potentially invalid due to improper TTT implementation (multi-epoch TTT before evaluation).

title: sdf
key_insights: Empty/test issue with no content.

title: [Question] Tokenizer is not counted in submission size
key_insights: The tokenizer is not counted in the 16MB submission size limit, which could give large-vocabulary models an artificial advantage by sneaking in information via tokenizer strings. The organizer ruled that exploiting this side-channel is 'not in the spirit of the competition' and will be evaluated case-by-case. Large vocab still incurs cost via the embedding table, partially mitigating the concern.

title: Community Tool: Parameter Golf Leaderboard Monitor (CLI + Claude Code Skill)
key_insights: A CLI tool and Claude Code skill for monitoring the leaderboard with zero dependencies. It includes heuristics to flag suspect submissions (score < 1.10 or val-only keywords) and can poll for live updates.

title: [Discussion] On the use of training on validation set
key_insights: Training on validation tokens before evaluating them is permitted only in a causal/online sense: you may train on tokens [0:N-1] before predicting token N, but not train on the validation set multiple times then re-evaluate. The author demonstrated that full validation-set fine-tuning can reduce BPB from ~1.23 to ~1.07, showing the evaluation budget is generous enough to enable near-overfitting if abused. The clarification closes the loophole of multi-pass validation fine-tuning.

title: Pausing on the competition until grant comes in
key_insights: A participant noted spending $550 in Modal compute credits in two days of sequential training jobs and had to pause. Highlights that GPU costs can escalate quickly with iterative experimentation.

title: Is PyTorch a hard requirement, or just the assumed default?
key_insights: PyTorch is not a hard requirement — JAX submissions are allowed as long as the record is independently reproducible from the code in the /records folder. The author demonstrates that PyTorch's poor support for complex ops creates a ~2.6x wallclock penalty for non-Transformer architectures like MEGALODON, potentially biasing the competition toward Llama-style designs rather than genuinely parameter-efficient alternatives.

title: Proposals for new rules to handle flood of submissions
key_insights: Proposes rules to handle submission flood from AI-assisted tools: limit one open record attempt per participant, merge similar concurrent submissions, and enforce stricter code quality. A commenter prototyped a structured evidence artifact system with a verifier to make the evidentiary surface explicit without changing submission rules.

title: Environment Clarifications (pytorch, CUDA, H100)
key_insights: The evaluation environment uses SXM H100s with the pre-installed packages from the RunPod template (no pip install needed). The requirements.txt torch version discrepancy was resolved by pinning to the template's installed version.

title: 10 min Submission requirements
key_insights: The 10-minute limit applies separately to training and evaluation — 10 minutes each, not 10 minutes total. This was clarified in the README after community confusion.

title: Experiment runner: harness, result logging, research_log.md
key_insights: Proposes building experiment infrastructure including a CLI-driven runner with config overrides, structured result logging, and config snapshots for reproducibility. Intended to streamline iterative experimentation for competition participants.

title: Modular package: break train_gpt_cpu.py into pgolf/ modules
key_insights: Proposes refactoring the monolithic training script into importable, testable modules covering config, data, model, optimizer, quantization, and evaluation. Aims to improve code quality, testability, and accessibility for contributors building on the baseline.

title: Project tooling: pyproject.toml, ruff, mypy, pytest
key_insights: Sets up project infrastructure with ruff, mypy, and pytest via uv-based dependency management. Purely tooling/CI setup with no direct ML technique relevance.

title: docs: research plan and hypotheses
key_insights: Establishes documentation structure including a competition plan and research log template for tracking experiments. No technical content visible in the issue itself.

title: Lets fkin goooooooooooooooo
key_insights: Enthusiasm post with no technical content or competition insights.

title: Run & track experiments on Hugging Face infra
key_insights: Provides an end-to-end reproducible setup for launching and tracking parameter-golf experiments on HF infrastructure using HF Jobs, Buckets, and Trackio for live metrics. The two-file setup (launch_job.py + train_job.py) simplifies experiment management and supports agentic/autoresearch workflows via HF skills. Could accelerate iteration speed for competition participants.

title: Add fixed-step and fixed-compute evaluation benchmarks for hardware accessibility
key_insights: Proposes adding fixed-step (N gradient steps) and fixed-compute (X FLOPs) evaluation modes alongside the existing H100 wall-clock benchmark to lower barriers for participants without H100 access and improve reproducibility. Suggests tiered leaderboard system (Bronze/Silver/Gold) or separate leaderboards for each mode.

title: ROCm Support PR Proposal
key_insights: Proposes adding ROCm support to the training script to allow participants with AMD hardware to iterate locally or on cheaper cloud instances, even though final submissions must run on 8xH100. This would lower the barrier for local development and testing.

title: Illegal submissions megathread
key_insights: A megathread clarifying rules around invalid submissions, particularly those generated by autoresearch tools or involving rules confusion. Important for understanding what evaluation methods and training procedures are permitted.

title: WEER FWE
key_insights: Spam/gibberish issue with no relevant content.

title: RunPod infrastructure costs: charged $8.65 for pods that never ran training
key_insights: Participants using RunPod are being charged for GPU provisioning time even when pods never boot, exhausting limited budgets before any training runs. Suggestions include a shared evaluation endpoint, compute credits for active participants, and a local single-GPU approximation mode.

title: Leaderboard adjustment
key_insights: Proposes adding a compression ratio metric (artifact_size * val_bpb / 16_000_000 - 1) to the leaderboard to better reflect the joint optimization of model size and prediction quality.

title: Int6 per-row scale clamp: quantization resolution vs. artifact compressibility tradeoff
key_insights: The clamp_min(1/31) in Int6 per-row quantization wastes levels for small-weight rows and becomes destructive at higher weight decay values, where the quantization gap can reach 0.114 BPB at WD=0.30. Removing the clamp improves quantization fidelity but dramatically increases artifact size due to wider scale entropy, suggesting quantized or grouped scale encodings as a promising research direction.

title: Why claude code ??
key_insights: Brief unexplained question about Claude Code with no additional context or follow-up. No actionable information provided.

title: Ternary UNet submission (PR #640) folder is in track_10min_16mb instead of track_non_record_16mb
key_insights: A 73.7M Ternary UNet submission was placed in the wrong directory (track_10min_16mb instead of track_non_record_16mb), despite being accepted only as a Notable Non-Record Run in the README. This is a repository organization/housekeeping issue that could cause leaderboard confusion.
